headline: Anterior cervical discectomy and fu — prior authorization (UHC)
denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

This letter advises you of our determination regarding your provider's request on 01/03/2026 for Anterior cervical discectomy and fusion C5-6 related to Cervical spondylosis with radiculopathy (M47.22). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines and Optum clinical criteria. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Continuation of Anterior cervical discectomy and fusion C5-6 requires an active prior authorization; the prior auth lapsed. A new authorization must be submitted.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with continuation of care lapsed. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination through our internal appeals process. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

This determination applies to your employer-sponsored commercial benefit while you remain covered under an active group plan (still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO).

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
clinical_context: The 72-year-old member has Cervical spondylosis with radiculopathy (M47.22). The member is still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO. She has been treated under commercial coverage with Anterior cervical discectomy and fusion C5-6 recommended as the next appropriate step. Arm weakness 4/5; EMG active radiculopathy; 8 weeks PT failed. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that continuation of care lapsed bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings (including dated scale scores in the chart) that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.
